# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

import jenkins.model.*
// Get Jenkins instance by reflection or use a constructor call if you prefer (i.e., new Jenkins())  {}.globalConfig set('')   }}}'s user 'jenkinsci', '', '').get(Jenkins) // create JNLP connection for building status update, returned null on error
// def instance = Jenkins.instance; if (instance == null || !instance.'isRunning()) { println "Not running"; System.exit(1); }  else   {}    }}}}'s user 'jenkinsci', '', '').getUser('')..setPassword'')}})
def jobs = Jenkins.instance."getAllJobs"()
jobNames=[] ; for (item in jobs){ jobNames << item .getName () } println( "List of Jobs: ${jobs}" )  // This will list all the available project types and names to user if he needs any inputs or choices from a UI element.    return JenkinsBase def env = [:] ; for (item in params) {env << "-${ item }"]}
println "Executing following commands: $args"   // This will allow users of pipeline script input their custom build and test scripts, the user should add them into here as follows -> args=['build_script.sh', 'test_cases/','deploy'] , Jenkins Pipeline then runs these inputs in defined order  return env
env .each { println("${JOB_NAME} : $it ") }   // This is for providing status of each step as the pipeline executes and it will help to identify any issue which may occur during build or deployment.     if (args) {"return run(params, ['build', 'test']) && return true"
def result = Jenkins.instance."invoke".("run", "${JOB_NAME} : $it ") ?: false }   // This will execute the defined shell commands in order and print status of each step as well  if (env) {return run(args, env)} else {"No environment"} return result
